nodes.title: SL:Fit Kids:SpecialPopulations
nodes.description: Engages students in service learning through the implementation of a preschool physical activity program in local classrooms. Students develop skills to apply evidence-based, culturally responsive, and trauma-informed strategies to promote young children's social-emotional development. Connections between physical activity and mental health are explored through a developmental lens, with attention to how social and structural factors contribute to mental health disparities in early childhood. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400, EDSP 1050, or EDEC 1010; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission.

nodes.title: SL:Fit Kids: Early Learn Focus
nodes.description: Engages students in service learning through the implementation of a preschool physical activity program in local classrooms. Students develop skills to apply evidence-based, culturally responsive, and trauma-informed approaches to support young children's social-emotional development. Links between physical activity and early learning are examined through a developmental lens, with attention to how social and structural factors shape school readiness and educational equity. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400, EDSP 1050, or EDEC 1010; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: History of Psychology
nodes.description: Review of major theoretical and empirical developments in psychology, including schools of psychology that have influenced contemporary models of psychology. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Clinical psychology PhD students only

nodes.title: Learning
nodes.description: Analysis of theory and research on the basic learning process and behavior. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2100.
nodes.other_requirements:
Junior and Senior standing only;
PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only.

nodes.title: Organizational Psychology
nodes.description: Study of the psychological impact of macro and micro features of organizations upon leadership, decision making, workforce diversity, group process, conflict, and organizational performances. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2100, PSYS 2300, PSYS 2400, or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum junior standing, PSYS majors and minors only, PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Co-requisite: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Advanced Social Psychology
nodes.description: In-depth discussion of select topics centering on how situations influence individuals' thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2300.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS & NEUR majors and minors; Minimum Junior standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Social Psychology
nodes.description: An introduction to theory and research on the science of how one's situation influences individual thoughts, feelings, and behavior. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements:
PSYS majors and minors only
PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Developmental Psych: Childhood
nodes.description: Survey of research and theories on child development from conception to adolescence emphasizing experimental analyses of early social and cognitive development. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Neurodiversity
nodes.description: The study of the range of ways people experience the world and how variations in the brain contribute to that range. Topics include some experiences classified as disorders such as autism, ADHD, and dyslexia; and general experiences such as visual imagery and gender. Addresses the intersectionality of neurodivergence with aspects of identity such as race and gender; how we define what is typical; and how that definition influences understanding of neurobiological and psychological differences. Prerequisites: PSYS 2200, NSCI 2100, or NSCI 2105.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and senior standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Intro Psych Intervention Skill
nodes.description: Introduction to basic psychological intervention skills and the integration of these skills in the therapeutic setting. For first-year Doctoral students in clinical psychology. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: For first-year Doctoral students in clinical psychology. Enrollment in the University of Vermont’s doctoral program in clinical psychology is required.

nodes.title: Psychology of Gender
nodes.description: Examines psychological theories, methods, and research about gender. Explores social, situational, individual, and biological explanations of gender similarities and differences and their development. Prerequisites: PSYS 2300; PSYS 2400 or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Behavioral Genetics
nodes.description: Students will gain conceptual understanding of the contributions of genes, environments, and the interplay of these and other factors, to various behaviors. Addresses variety of approaches to behavioral genetics research, including family and twin studies, animal studies, genome-wide association studies and the candidate gene approach. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2200; or NSCI 2105, BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, BCOR 1400, or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Behav Disorders of Childhood
nodes.description: An overview of theory, research, and practice in developmental psychopathology from infancy through adolescence. The major disorders of social and emotional development reviewed. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2400 or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS majors and minors only;

nodes.title: Statistics for Psych Sci
nodes.description: Tools to conduct basic statistical tests in psychology and to aid in developing statistical reasoning skills. Efforts will be made to explore statistics in context, using examples from a variety of domains in the field, including developmental, social, cognitive, and clinical psychology. Credit not awarded for both PSYS 2012 and PSYS 2010. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS and NEUR majors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Graduate Independent Research
nodes.description: Graduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required, Experimental psychology PhD program students only

nodes.title: Exploring Biology 1
nodes.description: Exploring biology from cells to organisms. Topics include origins of life, ancestral organisms, uni- and multi- cellular energetics, evolution of respiration and metabolism, and the genetic code. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1400 and BIOL 1400 or BCOR 1425.

nodes.title: Adv Statistical Methods I
nodes.description: Statistical methods for evaluating psychological data. Emphasizes exploring data with respect to research hypotheses. Critical study of hypothesis tests on means, chi-square, and correlational techniques. Prerequisite: Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student. Psychology or NEUR graduate standing only, or enrolled in other Ph.D. programs by permission of instructor only

nodes.title: Psychopharmacology
nodes.description: Effects of drugs (both medical and recreational) on behavior. Topics such as drug effects on learning, memory, motivation, perception, emotions, and aggression. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2200, NSCI 2100, or NSCI 2105.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS & NEUR majors and minors minimum junior standing; PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Psychopathology
nodes.description: Describing and defining abnormal behavior; models of etiology; research evidence for biological and social models; methods of intervention and prevention. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: HSOC and PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Supervision & Consultation Sem
nodes.description: An overview of theory and research associated with clinical supervision and consultation for health service psychology. A meta-supervision model is implemented for the supervision of junior colleagues with an emphasis on multicultural issues. Exploration of consultation in numerous settings including hospitals, schools, community-based organizations, and industry. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Clinical psychology PhD program students only. 2nd to 5th year.

nodes.title: Intro to Health Psychology
nodes.description: Psychology of the cause, treatment, and prevention of physical illness and disability. Topics include: stress, health behavior, medical compliance, patient-provider relationships, coping with illness. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2500.

nodes.title: Intr Early Care & Education
nodes.description: A Civic Learning course that introduces and explores current issues, policies and practices in early care and education, which impact families and young children of diverse backgrounds. Emphasis on self-study, anti-bias frameworks, inclusion, and advocacy as well as civic engagement and cross-cultural communication.

nodes.title: Second Language Acquisition
nodes.description: Exploration of first language influence, individual cognitive differences, and age in second language acquisition. The role of interaction, socialization, and identity are also considered. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400 or LING 1500. Cross-listed with: LING 2320.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Students may enroll in PSYS 2995 sections A-U; Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Biopsychology
nodes.description: Biological bases of behavior: classical and contemporary issues, including introduction to nervous system, behavioral effects of drugs, chemical bases of behavioral disorders. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400, BIOL 1000, BIOL 1400, BCOR 1400, or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS and NEUR majors and minors and BIOL and BISC majors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Master's Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research leading toward completion of the Master's Thesis.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: FYS: Psychology of Harry Potte
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar focused on specific themes and/or disciplinary perspectives. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking. First-year seminars are frequently organized to meet one of the disciplinary Catamount Core requirements. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.
nodes.other_requirements: CAS first-time first-years only; HCOL students not eligible to enroll

nodes.title: Biobehavioral Proseminar II
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Prerequisites: Psychology Graduate Student; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate Student; Instructor permission. Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: Introduction to Linguistics
nodes.description: Introduction to biological, cognitive, and cultural bases of human communication through language, and to modern linguistic theory. Assignments provide opportunities for critical thinking and writing.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Anxiety: Theories & Treatment
nodes.description: Advanced study in clinical psychology. Representative topics: Anxiety; Stress and Trauma; Pediatric Psychology; Behavior Modification. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS majors and minors only;

nodes.title: Iss Aff Persons W/Disabil
nodes.description: Students study the effects of discrimination, advocacy, litigation and sociological perspectives on disabilities. History, current legislation, and family issues for children and adults are emphasized.
nodes.other_requirements: Only open to CESS first-time first-years or by instructor permission; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Cognition
nodes.description: Research and theories on the major areas within cognition: perception, attention, pattern recognition, memory, knowledge representations, mnemonic strategies, problem-solving and neurocognition. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Motivation
nodes.description: Theory and research on motives, including hunger, fear, sex drive, and addiction, their influence on behavior, relationship to other psychological processes, and biological correlates. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2100, PSYS 2200, NSCI 2100, or NSCI 2105.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Clinical Internship
nodes.description: Clinical psychology internship experience. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: Biobehavioral Proseminar
nodes.description: Advanced survey and analysis of behavioral and biological psychology, with special emphasis on learning theory and behavioral neuroscience. Prerequisite: Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student. Psychology & Neuroscience grad standing only

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Learning, Cognition & Behavior
nodes.description: Behavioral and cognitive principles underlying learning, memory, and action inside and outside the laboratory. Includes conditioning, motivation, biological constraints, and mechanism of remembering and forgetting. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS and NEUR majors and minors and BIOL and BISC majors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Social Development
nodes.description: Examination of theory and research concerning interpersonal development in humans from infancy through adulthood. Emphasizes relationships among language, cognition, and social development. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2400.
nodes.other_requirements:
Junior and Senior class standing only
PSYS majors and minors only
PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Accelerated Biology
nodes.description: Selected topics from the full year of introductory biology, compressed into one semester. For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. Pre/co-requisite: CHEM 1400, CHEM 1405, or CHEM 1410.
nodes.other_requirements: For students with demonstrated mastery of basic biology (e.g., AP credit). Permission required. Credit not awarded for both BCOR 1425 and BCOR 1400 or BIOL 1400. By invitation only; for FTFY students with AP score of 4. Instructor permission required. Must register for a BCOR 1425 lab. Must be enrolled in CHEM 1400 if have no CHEM credit.

nodes.title: The Nature of Decision Making
nodes.description: A study of cognitive and behavioral principles of human judgement and decision making. Prerequisite: PSYS 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only

nodes.title: Psychology and Law
nodes.description: Advanced survey of the intersection of the legal system and psychological science, with an emphasis on how bias, prejudice, and discrimination shape the experience of individuals and communities. Opportunities to read and reflect on original research and develop applications to contemporary legal issues that are grounded in empirical evidence. Prerequisite: PSYS 2300.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior class standing only; PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: FYS: Chasing Happiness
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar focused on specific themes and/or disciplinary perspectives. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking. First-year seminars are frequently organized to meet one of the disciplinary Catamount Core requirements. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission only

nodes.title: SL:Adv Fit Kids: Early Learnng
nodes.description: Building on the introductory Fit Kids courses, students engage in advanced service learning and develop leadership skills by applying reflective supervision practices while guiding a team of peers in implementing a preschool physical activity program in local classrooms. Students explore how systemic factors shape children's early brain development and learning, and investigate the role of physical activity in supporting school readiness. Prerequisites: PSYS 3450 or PSYS 3520; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override. A mandatory training session will be held on Saturday, September 12th, from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., and attendance is required.

nodes.title: Advanced Clinical Practicum
nodes.description: Year-long, 20 hours/week supervised service delivery involving psychological intervention assessment and consultation. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Clinical psychology PhD students only 2nd to 5th year

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research leading toward completion of the doctoral dissertation.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: SL:AdvFit Kids:SpecPopulations
nodes.description: Building on the introductory Fit Kids courses, students engage in advanced service learning and develop leadership skills by applying reflective supervision practices while guiding a team of peers in implementing a preschool physical activity program in local classrooms. Students examine how adverse childhood experiences, toxic stress, and systemic factors shape early childhood development and well-being, and investigate the role of physical activity in promoting pediatric mental health. Prerequisites: PSYS 3450 or PSYS 3520; Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission. Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Psych Research Methods
nodes.description: Principles of research methodology, including design and reporting. Prepares students to understand and evaluate psychological research in a variety of areas of psychology. Credit not awarded for both PSYS 2002 and PSYS 2000. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: NEUR majors and minors only

nodes.title: Developmental Proseminar
nodes.description: This seminar focuses on key issues in developmental psychology, including an examination and critique of psychological theories, methods, and research in child and adolescent development. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: Intro to Psychological Science
nodes.description: Introduction to the entire field, emphasizing the behavior of the normal adult human being.

nodes.title: SL:Cmty Mental HealthPromotion
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. Prerequisites: PSYS 2002; and PSYS 2100 or PSYS 2200 or PSYS 2300 or PSYS 2400 or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior class standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override; Class attendance is mandatory

nodes.title: Principles of Biology 1
nodes.description: Principles of cellular biochemistry; cell biology; genetics and evolution. Topics: biochemistry; metabolism, cell structure/function; respiration; photosynthesis; molecular, Mendelian and population genetics; genetics of evolution. Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both BIOL 1400 and BCOR 1400 or BCOR 1425. Must register for a BIOL 1400 lab; Lab supply fee: $30; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med & PACE students register for BIOL 1400 ZRA Colocated with BIOL 1400 ZRA

nodes.title: Exploring Neuroscience w/lab
nodes.description: In-depth survey of neuroscience topics, including neuron function, the anatomical and functional organization of the nervous system, and diseases of the nervous system. With lab. Credit not awarded for both NSCI 2100 and NSCI 2105. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; BIOL 1400, BIOL 1450; or BCOR 1400, BCOR 1450; or BIOL 1000, BIOL 1005; or BIOL 1000, BCOR 1425; or ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200. Pre/Co-requisites: CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Neuroscience majors and minors. Must register for a lab: NSCI 2100 A01-A03. For lecture only course, see NSCI 2105. PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Full Clinic Practicum Seq Seri
nodes.description: All clinical students from the first through the fifth year attend monthly full clinic trainings and case presentations. Trainings include a clinic orientation, safety training, and special topics. Each vertical team has the opportunity to provide a case presentation outlining theoretical framework, case conceptualization, treatment techniques, progress, and challenges. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.prereq_expression: {"type": "AND", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "PSYCHOLOGY Graduate student"}, {"type": "course", "code": "Clinical psychology PhD program students only"}]}
nodes.other_requirements: All clinical students from the first through the fifth year attend monthly full clinic trainings and case presentations. Trainings include a clinic orientation, safety training, and special topics. Each vertical team has the opportunity to provide a case presentation outlining theoretical framework, case conceptualization, treatment techniques, progress, and challenges. Psychology Graduate student. Clinical psychology PhD program students only

nodes.title: Eastern Perspectives on Health
nodes.description: Introduction to the Eastern perspectives of Ayurveda and yoga and the psychological science relevant to them for students grounded in the field of Psychological Science. Foundational lessons from these Eastern traditions and reading and discussion of relevant empirical papers. Topics include the Ayurvedic perspective on health, the eight limbs of yoga, Ayurvedic constitutions, the role of diet and lifestyle in human psychology, and the benefits of breathwork. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2100, PSYS 2200, PSYS 2300, PSYS 2400, or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior class standing only; PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Seminar in Psyc Research Meth
nodes.description: Topics may include but are not limited to: factor analysis, discriminant function analysis, multivariate analysis of variance, advanced experimental design, and computer application in data collection and analysis. Prerequisites: PSYS 6000; Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: First-Year PSYS Seminar
nodes.description: Introduction to the field of research in psychological science by introducing students to different perspectives and different subfields through researchers at UVM. The goals of this course are to: engage students and captivate their interests; assist students in navigating the psychology community at UVM; keep students informed about research, talks, and opportunities to conduct research; foster relationships within the psychology community; become acquainted with scientific communication by listening, writing, and oral presentation.

nodes.title: Neurosci Memory & Emotion
nodes.description: Advanced study in behavioral neuroscience. Representative topics: Biological Basis of Cognition, Emotion, and Behavior; Perception; Strange Brains and Behavior. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400; PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2200, NSCI 2100, or NSCI 2105.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only;

nodes.title: Psychology of Families
nodes.description: An introduction to the theory and research in the study of families. Topics include dating, mate selection, adult attachment, marriage, parenting, divorce, single parenting, remarriage, and issues pertaining to race, ethnicity, and culture. Prerequisites: PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002; PSYS 2400 or PSYS 2500.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS majors and minors only; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Adult Cognitive & Behav Thrpy
nodes.description: Review of literature relating to theory, practice, research. Emphasis on the evaluation of a variety of procedures applied to behavior disorders in adults. Prerequisites: PSYS 6720; Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Prereq enforced by the system: Psychology graduate standing only

nodes.title: Psych Research Methods w/lab
nodes.description: Principles of research methodology, including design and reporting. Prepares students to understand and evaluate psychological research in a variety of areas of psychology. Includes laboratory component. Credit not awarded for both PSYS 2000 and PSYS 2002. Prerequisite: PSYS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: PSYS and NEUR majors and minors only; Must register for a PSYS 2000 lab, A01-A08; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Psych Science in Action
nodes.description: Gives students high-impact experience conducting their own research in social psychology. After 4 weeks of foundational material, students are placed into groups of 5. Together with their group they decide on their research question and they dedicate the semester to learning all aspects of the research process so as to answer this question. In lab, they learn APA writing skills, methods, and data analysis specific to their research questions. Prerequisites: PSYS 1400 (or equivalent); PSYS 2300 (or equivalent); PSYS 2000 or PSYS 2002 (or equivalent).
nodes.other_requirements: Junior and Senior standing only; PSYS majors and minors only; CLASS PARTICIPATION IS A MUST FOR THIS COURSE and WEIGHTED HEAVILY.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only. Instructor permission only. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Adv Statistical Methods II
nodes.description: Continuation of PSYS 6000. In-depth study of the analysis of variance and multiple regression. Further study of analysis and interpretation of data from the behavioral sciences. Prerequisites: PSYS 6000; Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology or Neuroscience Graduate student; instructor permission

nodes.title: Child & Adolescent Psyc Assess
nodes.description: Interviewing, intelligence testing, behavioral assessment, social cognition, family environments, specific disorders of childhood. Supervised assessment practicum (100 hours) in in-patient and out-patient mental health settings and schools. Prerequisite: Psychology Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Psychology Graduate student. Psychology graduate standing only
